digest.top_line: BART: adjuvant pelvic RT post-cystectomy cuts 2yr locoregional failure (LRFFS 87.1% vs 76.0%, HR 0.43, p=0.04); OS 70.4 vs 57.4% ns.
digest.tldr: Bladder carried the day: BART, the first RCT of adjuvant pelvic RT after cystectomy, halves locoregional failure (HR 0.43), with benefit concentrated in T3+/N+ disease and no isolated locoregional recurrences in the RT arm. OS ns, underpowered, and predates adjuvant nivolumab.

studies.name: BART
studies.tldr: 2yr locoregional FFS 87.1% vs 76.0% (HR 0.43, p=0.04) with adjuvant pelvic RT after cystectomy; OS 70.4 vs 57.4% ns.

details.text: 📊 Efficacy by endpoint at 2yr (adjuvant RT vs observation)
🔍 Phase III multicenter RCT, N=153 (RT 77, obs 76), accrued 2016-2024, 1:1

details: 📐 Adjuvant RT 50.4Gy/28fx to cystectomy bed + pelvic nodes, stoma-sparing IMRT, daily IGRT

details.text: 📊 LRFFS benefit concentrates in node-positive / T3+ (HR, RT vs obs)

details: 📊 No isolated locoregional recurrences in the RT arm (pattern of failure)

subdetails: Acute G2 GI higher with RT: 17.5% vs 1.4%, no discontinuation

details: ⚠️ 1° EP is locoregional FFS (surrogate); OS ns (HR 0.78, p=0.31), trial underpowered
⚠️ No immunotherapy given; predates adjuvant nivolumab SOC, RT+IO combos untested here

verdict.rationale: Randomised phase 3, prespecified LRFFS primary hit; contests observation-only default after cystectomy. OS ns and sample-size short, but the locoregional divergence is internally valid.
verdict.audience: High-risk MIBC (pT3-4, N+, or R+), post-cystectomy + chemo, median age 57

studies.significance: The RT read is in the per-protocol and node-positive subgroups: PP 2yr LRFFS 93.2% vs 75.0% (HR 0.27), N+ HR 0.22 (0.06-0.75). Benefit concentrates in N+/T3+ disease. Standard 50.4Gy/28fx IMRT with no excess late G3+ toxicity (8.4 vs 10.5%), so this moves the adjuvant-RT decision for N+ MIBC.
studies.monday_clinic: In node-positive or T3+ urothelial MIBC after radical cystectomy and cisplatin chemo, this supports adjuvant pelvic RT as a locoregional-control option (not an established OS benefit); it does not extend to immunotherapy-treated pts, who were absent here.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Phase 3 multicenter open-label RCT, 1:1, **N=153** (RT 77, obs 76), accrued 2016-2024; stratified by nodal stage (N0 vs N+) and chemotherapy. Median follow-up 47 mo. **Fell short of its sample-size goal.**

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: High-risk (**T3-4, N1-3, or R+**) non-metastatic urothelial MIBC post-radical cystectomy. 62% pT3-4, 41% pN+, 28% variant histology; median age 57, median 20 nodes dissected, 4.6% positive margins. Chemo neoadjuvant 71% / adjuvant 20%; **no immunotherapy.**

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: **50.4 Gy in 28 fractions** to cystectomy bed plus pelvic nodes (common / internal / external iliac, presacral, obturator) via stoma- and bowel-sparing **IMRT** with daily onboard image guidance.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary:** 2-yr locoregional failure-free survival. Secondary: bladder-cancer-specific survival, DFS, OS. Fine-Gray competing-risk analysis applied to LRFFS and DFS.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: **Primary endpoint met**; secondary DFS, BCSS, and OS all favored adjuvant RT numerically but did not reach significance. Per-arm effect sizes are in the endpoint table above.

analysis_sections.label: Safety
analysis_sections.body: Late grade 3+ toxicity similar: **8.4% (RT) vs 10.5% (obs), p=0.60**. Acute grade 2 GI higher with RT (17.5% vs 1.4%) with **no toxicity-related discontinuation**; acute grade 3 GI 1.6% vs 4.1%.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents high-risk (T3-4 / N+ / R+) urothelial MIBC after radical cystectomy and cisplatin-based chemotherapy. Does not represent immunotherapy-treated pts or low-risk organ-confined, node-negative, margin-negative disease.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Billed as the largest RCT of adjuvant RT in this setting. Predates adjuvant nivolumab (CheckMate-274) as SOC, so no RT+IO overlap was tested. An **OS** signal is deferred to the planned **MERCY** individual-patient-data meta-analysis.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Open-label; primary is a locoregional surrogate (OS ns, underpowered after missing accrual). Median age 57 and 71% neoadjuvant-chemo cohort may limit transfer to older Western MIBC practice.

open_questions: Does adjuvant RT improve overall survival (MERCY IPD meta-analysis pending)
Efficacy of adjuvant RT combined with adjuvant immunotherapy
Optimal patient selection beyond N+ and T3+ high-risk criteria

related_trials.brief_title: Adjuvant Radiotherapy in Patients With Pathological High-risk Bladder Cancer (GETUG-AFU 30)
related_trials.brief_summary:
This is a randomized multicentre study in patients with high-risk MIBC to investigate adjuvant radiotherapy after radical cystectomy and pelvic lymph node dissection.

The objective of the study is to provide evidence that adjuvant radiotherapy improves loco-regional control with potential benefits in survival. The study will also evaluate the quality of life of patients and the tolerance of the treatment.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:

related_trials.brief_title: Adjuvant Nivolumab Following Chemo-Radiation in Localized Muscle-Invasive Bladder Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This is a phase 2, single arm, open label trial to evaluate the rate of failure free survival at 2 years after start of chemoradiation with adjuvant nivolumab in adult subjects who undergo chemoradiation for localized bladder cancer.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:

related_trials.brief_title: Atezolizumab After Chemo-radiotherapy for MIBC Patients Not Eligible for Radical Cystectomy
related_trials.brief_summary:
Patients older than ≥18 years, with muscle-invasive bladder cancer unfit for radical cystectomy because of age, comorbidities, and/or patient's refusal.

This study is designed as a multicentre, single-arm phase II study.

related_trials.brief_title: Adjuvant Radiotherapy After Cystectomy for Muscle Invasive Bladder Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: A radical cystectomy + extended pelvic lymph node dissection is considered to be the treatment of choice for patients with muscle invasive bladder cancer (MIBC). Despite this aggressive treatment the outcome is poor and ultimately, 30% of the patients with ≥pT3 tumors develop a pelvic recurrence. One- and 2-years survival for patients developing a local recurrence after cystectomy is only 8% and 3% respectively, with a median survival of \<4 months. For patients with lymph node recurrence…
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria: muscle invasive bladder cancer with:

* ≥ pathological tumor stage (p)T3 stage + presence of lymphovascular invasion on pathological examination
* pT4
* \<10 lymph nodes removed
* positive lymph nodes
* positive surgical margins

Exclusion Criteria:
